title: Service project WBS concept
overview: Describes how a WBS applies to projects whose outcome is a service.
detailed_summary: A service project will have a WBS documenting all components associated with the service offering a customer can experience. Examples include wedding planning services, cleaning services, audit services, and repair services. The WBS can act as a checklist of deliverables used to configure the service and can include interim deliverables associated with providing the service.
content: Service projects still produce deliverables, even when the final outcome is experiential rather than physical. In such projects, the WBS documents service components and associated outputs that must be prepared or delivered to the customer. The document notes that these can function as a checklist of service-related deliverables and can include tangible outputs or interim deliverables needed to provide the service effectively.
relationships: Service projects use WBS to define service-related outputs and configuration elements
search_queries: How does a WBS work for service projects?
Can a service have a WBS?
What are service deliverables in a WBS?
common_questions: Do service projects need a WBS?
What does a service WBS document?
Can customer-experience elements be WBS deliverables?
can_answer: How services are represented in a WBS
What service WBS items can include
examples: A wedding planning service WBS can include Ceremony, Reception, and Transportation deliverables.
best_practices: Treat service components and configuration outputs as deliverables.
Use the WBS as a checklist to prevent service elements from being overlooked.
warnings: Assuming a service has no deliverables can create planning and budgeting omissions.
limitations: Service deliverables may be less visually tangible than product components and require careful wording.
troubleshooting: If stakeholders struggle to define service deliverables, ask what the customer will receive, experience, or rely on.
decision_rules: If a service component or associated output is needed to provide the service, include it in the WBS.
